title: Tax Relief from Tariffs and High Costs Act
short_title: Tax Relief from Tariffs and High Costs Act
plain_summary: This bill creates a special tax credit for 2025 that helps people save money on their taxes. If you qualify, you can get back 10 percent of what you owe in federal income taxes. To get this credit, you must earn less than $100,000 if you are single or less than $200,000 if you are married. The bill is meant to help people deal with high costs and expensive tariffs.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Tax Relief from Tariffs and High Costs Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Refundable Credit for Portion of Individual Federal Income Tax Liability for 2025
sections.text: This section changes the tax code to create a new tax credit for individual taxpayers. For tax years that begin after December thirty first, two thousand twenty four, and before January first, two thousand twenty six, people can get a credit equal to ten percent of their federal income tax bill. However, this credit is not available to taxpayers whose modified adjusted gross income is more than one hundred thousand dollars. For married couples filing together, the limit is two hundred thousand dollars. Modified adjusted gross income is a measure of income calculated using rules already in the tax code. Federal income tax liability means the amount of tax a person owes after subtracting certain basic credits but before applying refundable credits like this new one. The section also makes technical changes to other parts of the tax code to include this new credit in the proper lists and tables. These changes take effect for tax years beginning after December thirty first, two thousand twenty four.
